conventions.testing: Unit tests with cargo test, integration tests
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation

constraints: should integrate with existing pattern system
test_coverage: Good unit and integration test coverage, with explicit test documenting multi-line limitation

library_findings.notes: Core tree-sitter Rust bindings for AST parsing
Language-specific grammar for Rust code analysis
Language-specific grammar for Python code analysis
Language-specific grammar for JavaScript code analysis

approaches.name: Hybrid Approach
approaches.description: Combine regex patterns with tree-sitter AST analysis. Use regex for simple patterns and tree-sitter for complex multi-line patterns. Maintain backward compatibility by keeping existing pattern system.
pros: Best of both worlds: regex speed + AST accuracy

approaches.name: Full AST Migration
approaches.description: Replace regex patterns entirely with tree-sitter queries. Convert all existing patterns to AST-based queries and deprecate regex system.

approaches.name: Parallel Analysis
approaches.description: Run both regex and AST analysis in parallel, merge results. Use tree-sitter as enhancement layer while keeping regex as baseline.

recommendation: Hybrid approach offers the best balance of backward compatibility and improved detection capabilities. Start with parallel analysis for key multi-line patterns (SQL injection, command injection), then gradually migrate more patterns to AST-based analysis as confidence grows.
